column6:
The early tiles and dry protection for those tiles was good design. The flare system needed work. Locking it behind frequency per quest was fine to prevent teams from abusing it, but it needed to unlock after X amount of real hours so that teams didn't get stuck on a tile for 77 hours. Being stuck on the first ToA tile for over 3 real-life days was an actually miserable experience that completely soured my attitude about this event, and future small-team events moving forward. Given some of the elitist responses I heard about from other members of my team expressing similar sentiments, I don't have much faith in the event team when it comes to actually balancing these types of events moving forward.

Clearly, these events are aimed to cater to a more elite category of pvmer with access to better gear who can push raids at higher levels and clear them faster.
column7: I'm sure there was, but it was largely over-shadowed by how negative the experience was at the end. By the time the event ended, I was only participating because I didn't want to receive an event strike, not because I was having fun.
column9:
The lore and story were fun, and the quest descriptions were humorous. The bot and palico were a cool addition. The balancing on team composition was poor, and the inconsistency between some tiles being "dry protected" while others weren't was awful design when combined with a flawed flare system that allowed teams that were ahead to continue using them while teams that were actually stuck and could have used a flare had no recourse because they needed to flare "recently".

I know my criticism comes across as "salty," and I'm sure some of it is salt-induced. It's not about "losing," it's about the way we lost. This event was pitched as having a way to avoid getting stuck on tiles, and then the system that allegedly fixed that issue did nothing to stop my team from being stuck on the same frustrating tile for over 3 days. We ended the event no closer to finishing that tile than when we first started the quest. That is objectively bad design when you consider how many hours our team spent trying to make progress.

Find a better balance between luck vs effort/time participating. This event gets 2/5 stars for me, I would not want to do it again without changes to the event mechanics and several quest tiles.

column6:
Positives: 

- Event was well organised and structured. Staff were prompt and receptive.
- The lore was fun (I recommend continuing doing themed bingos like this).
- Good mix of content in the bingo tiles. Plenty of opportunities to learn new content, and even re-attempt known content with weird gear (which definitely added to the fun). Definitely recommend continuing with gear restrictions in the future. 
- Good balance of kc & rng tiles. I would actually suggest leaning into rng a bit more as the event progresses, as it gives teams a chance to catch up to the top teams who are just playing 24/7. The x points/shards/kc or unique tiles are really good, more of those!


Negatives:

- Wildy RNG tile/s. Will only speak for myself (but I would imagine I am not alone), but I find wildy content to be really triggering. Understand that there is PvM content in the wildy that you would want covered in a bingo, but maybe keep it limited to KC only. 
- Team balance. To be honest I don't really know how you could do better in this space without increasing team size, this would have been challenging for you with teams of 4. From a competition standpoint, only a handful of teams could actually win this bingo as the scaling difficulty of tiles starts to eliminate players from contention. It felt like as the bingo went on that the entire event was placed on 1-2 people's shoulders on any given team, which in my case as one of those players was kind of stressful and made me feel like I had to play more than I wanted to. 
- Changing the CoX tile mid event. Understand this was just a mistake, but it was kind of bad optics as it appeared to assist 1 team more than others. 
- Flares. Not entirely sure how to explain how I feel about the flares, but from my perspective I don't think the system worked as intended. There were some weird things going on with flares, one example that comes to mind was when 3x teams flared the TOA unique tile, and for some reason the 1st flare got ignored and the 2nd/3rd flares got instantly taken. It just seemed shady. Also, from a competitive standpoint, there is no real incentive in taking another team's flair to help them. You essentially had to be on the same tile that someone else was struggling on, otherwise you would be hampering your team by not working on your active tile. There definitely needs to be a help system in place for team's that are struggling on a tile, but this one could have been better.
column7: No specific moment, I would say the event as a whole was great fun (with the exception of wildy). It was great to see teammates and even other teams complete new content for the first time.
column9: Will leave all of my feedback in the positives/negatives section. Might just use this one to say thank you to the event staff for all the effort preparing and managing the bingo. All in all, it was a very fun event and I look forward to seeing what new ideas you have for future events. Cheers!

column9:
I'm a sucker for a theme and flavor but found some of the lore and such to be a bit excessive. For instance the flavor text for each quest was just extra words that I was skimming over while looking for the important info.

The details and information for the event could be organized and presented better. I found myself bouncing between the FAQ channel, the announcements channel, and the rules channel looking for the information I wanted.

Fashion Hunter and GemGram are fun additions for a social clan vibe and help provide additional fun without hurting anyone, +1 from me.

Having Refs/Mentors step in to help with teams near the bottom was awesome and still probably works well even with larger teams. A helping hand when struggling is a huge mental boost as long as it doesn't ruin the competition.

Flares were a good idea on paper to avoid teams getting stuck but in practice I think they damage the integrity of the competition. Bingo participants being able to take flares from other teams means they are either taking 1m to help another team that is at the same point in the event (sometimes at a disadvantage to themselves) or focusing on content other than their current quest. Some teams waited hours or longer for someone to pick up their flare whereas others made plans with a friend in the clan to pick up their flare immediately. Weaker teams are afraid of using their flare for fear of actually getting stuck later but any strong team with a real chance of winning the event should have been flaring often just for the increase in completion speed. Generally speaking I think any kind of helping-hand mechanic like this should somehow be locked behind being actually stuck or significantly behind other teams.

Check-ins and words of encouragement from refs are welcome but messages that are obviously copy-pasted into multiple team channels just feel hollow and inauthentic.

The requests for sharing the current standings during the event should have just been flatly declined or actually honored. I got the impression you guys were afraid to share the exact details for fear of the bottom teams getting discouraged but I find it silly to dance around the topic and then release only a partial answer. Either put your foot down and say that you think the experience is better when you're focused on only your team's progress or let people see the standings and learn to manage their shitty attitudes about not winning.

I might just be being a wet blanket but spammy/unserious messages going in the event's announcement channel is a pet peeve.

With the freedom of a bot-driven event I would have hoped to see some actual "twists and turns". The event was still great and well-crafted, perhaps just the marketing for the event got a little overzealous.

It seems like the event length was pretty much perfect and lined up very well with how long it took teams to complete most/all of the quests.

I don't think I have much more to say without knowing how EG members normally interact with events and what kind of goals yall have.

I hope nothing comes off as super critical, just sharing the thoughts I have. I really enjoyed the event and am impressed with the effort to make it happen.

column9:
I am really sad and upset about the strike I received, and I also consider it unfair. I was not initially going to enter this bingo, but I was convinced to join. Although I participated a lot every day, we also lost days because we died often to Yama, and had many more days where we were just unlucky.

Just because the Wise Old Man didn't rise the points, doesn't mean I didn't help the team. With the basic requirements unmet (trident, RC, and a whip), the strike leaves me with a sour, bad taste and makes me not want to enter any more bingos or events at all.

This event should have been categorized as a hard event and not for starters to PVM. Even after all this, I hope you open more events for PVM starters and don't backlash them with strikes. PVM starters are at the front lines of the cannon, trying new encounters. They are here to escape from reality, have fun, and try new things—not to get strikes.
